commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps Signal container REST success-body `JSON.parse` in a stable malformed-JSON error and tightens the existing unit test assertion.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests 0. Total +4 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reads a mocked malformed success body in the existing edge-case test and then reaches a bare `JSON.parse(text)`. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.

target.title: fix(signal): guard containerRestRequest JSON.parse against malformed responses
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/signal/src/client-container.ts:262` calls `JSON.parse(text)` without try/catch. The `containerRestRequest` function is the main REST entry point for the Signal container (used by send, typing, version, etc.). A malformed JSON response from the container causes an unhandled `SyntaxError`.

Note: the success body is already bounded by `readProviderTextResponse` (16 MiB cap, D1 protected). This PR adds D4 crash protection (try/catch).

## Changes

- `extensions/signal/src/client-container.ts`: wrap `JSON.parse(text)` in try/catch → `Error("Signal REST returned malformed JSON")`
- `extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts`: tighten existing "handles JSON parse errors gracefully" test to assert the specific error message

**Scope**: `containerRestRequest` success path only (1 line). The test already existed.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Unit test — already exists, tightened assertion

```
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts

 ✓ containerRestRequest edge cases > handles JSON parse errors gracefully
 ... (all existing tests pass)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
```

### Before/after

```
// Before
return JSON.parse(text) as T;  // 💥 SyntaxError

// After
try { return JSON.parse(text) as T; }
catch { throw new Error("Signal REST returned malformed JSON"); }
```
